mechanism_narrative: Declining unemployment rate signals labor market tightening and rising wage pressures, which compress corporate profit margins and reduce equity valuations. A sustained monthly decline in UNRATE over 3 months predicts negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days as markets reprice earnings expectations downward.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE declines by more than 0.2 percentage points over 3 consecutive months
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 10.2% as of 2009-10, elevated post-crisis level
regime_hint: labor market recovery phase with wage acceleration risk
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 55% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: The federal funds rate at near-zero levels (0.12%) combined with sustained low rates signals an extended monetary accommodation regime. This mechanism predicts positive S&P 500 returns as low discount rates support equity valuations and encourage risk-on positioning. The signal remains active as long as FEDFUNDS remains below 0.5%.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS remains below 0.5% for at least 2 consecutive months
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 0.12% as of 2009-10, near zero-bound
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 60-day returns in more than 50% of triggered instances, or correlation between FEDFUNDS level and forward returns becomes statistically insignificant (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Rising retail sales (RSAFS) combined with increasing housing starts (HOUST) signals strengthening consumer demand and construction activity. This joint expansion in demand indicators predicts positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days as earnings growth accelerates. The mechanism requires both series to show month-over-month growth simultaneously.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS and HOUST both increase month-over-month for 2 consecutive months
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 590k units, RSAFS at $344.7B as of 2009-09
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in more than 52% of triggered instances despite joint demand growth, or interaction term coefficient becomes statistically insignificant (p > 0.15).

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) declines signal weakening manufacturing capacity utilization and reduced business investment. A 2-month consecutive decline in INDPRO predicts negative S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days as markets immediately reprice cyclical earnings expectations downward in response to supply-side deterioration.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 98.46 as of 2009-09, near trough levels
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in more than 58% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.01 over any 1-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1) measured quarterly combined with employment growth (PAYEMS) measured monthly creates a composite demand signal. When both indicators show positive growth momentum simultaneously, equity valuations expand as earnings growth accelerates. This interaction-driven mechanism predicts positive 60-day returns when both series trend upward.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS increases month-over-month while GDPC1 growth rate (annualized) exceeds 1.5% in latest quarter
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 at $13,014B as of 2009-08, PAYEMS at 130,848k as of 2009-10
regime_hint: early-cycle expansion with synchronized demand growth
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 60-day returns in more than 48% of triggered instances despite synchronized growth, or interaction coefficient becomes insignificant (p > 0.20).

mechanism_narrative: Inflation measured by CPIAUCSL rising above 2.5% year-over-year combined with FEDFUNDS remaining below 1% signals a monetary policy lag where real rates are negative. This regime predicts negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days as markets anticipate future rate hikes that will compress valuations and reduce real asset returns.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL year-over-year change exceeds 2.5% while FEDFUNDS remains below 1.0%
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL at 215.79 as of 2009-09, FEDFUNDS at 0.12% as of 2009-10
regime_hint: negative real rates with inflation acceleration risk
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 55% of triggered instances despite negative real rate signal, or interaction term fails to improve model fit (R² improvement < 0.02).

mechanism_narrative: Payroll employment (PAYEMS) shows a sustained 3-month uptrend indicating labor market recovery and rising consumer income. This always-on trend signal predicts positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days as improved employment supports consumer spending and corporate earnings growth throughout the quarter.
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS at 130,848k as of 2009-10, recovering from crisis lows
regime_hint: any regime with positive employment momentum
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 60-day returns in more than 52% of all periods, or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) combined with payroll employment (PAYEMS) both rising simultaneously signals broad-based economic recovery with expanding production capacity and labor utilization. This interaction-driven mechanism predicts positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days as synchronized supply and labor growth supports earnings expansion and reduces recession risk.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO increases month-over-month and PAYEMS increases month-over-month for 2 consecutive months
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 98.46 and PAYEMS at 130,848k as of 2009-10
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in more than 50% of triggered instances despite synchronized supply-labor growth, or interaction coefficient becomes statistically insignificant (p > 0.18).
